refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time by optimizing load, travel, and dump phases to meet the customer-defined cycle time target.
refined_fr.rationale: Decomposing the overall cycle time KPI into sub-functions targeting each phase of the cycle allows focused design and verification of each segment, improving clarity and traceability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time for load, travel, and dump phases individually and in aggregate under defined duty cycle and site conditions.

candidate_dps: Optimize vehicle mechanical and control systems for load, travel, and dump efficiency
Implement teleoperation support to reduce operator delays
Integrate safety services to avoid cycle interruptions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize vehicle mechanical and control systems for load, travel, and dump efficiency

potential_sources: Interdependence between load and travel phases timing
Safety service interventions affecting cycle phases
coupling_check.notes: DP focuses on vehicle optimization, minimizing cross-domain coupling by isolating teleoperation and safety as separate concerns.

assumptions_risks: Vehicle mechanical improvements can achieve cycle time targets without excessive cost or complexity
Teleoperation and safety services will not introduce delays beyond acceptable thresholds
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit DP for teleoperation and safety integration in next decomposition level to address coupling risks.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and control systems responsible for load, travel, and dump operations

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends operational data for monitoring and analysis

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands from teleoperation or higher-level control

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges safety status and alerts with Safety Services

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of vehicle-specific cycle time functions
High containment of cycle time related physical and control functions, minimal diffusion
Safety functions are critical but cycle time optimization is not primary scope

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning cycle time optimization DP to Vehicle module centralizes responsibility and minimizes diffusion, enabling focused improvements.
containment_risks: Potential delays caused by teleoperation or safety interventions not fully captured within Vehicle module
Cross-module coordination required for real-time cycle time optimization
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and monitoring to manage cross-module interactions impacting cycle time.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes cycle time KPI into manageable sub-functions; DP selection logically addresses primary physical optimization.

diffusion_risks: Cycle time improvements may be diffused if teleoperation or safety modules intervene unexpectedly
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for selected DP; interfaces to other modules are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring cycle times and monitoring interface data flows.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of cycle time optimization to Vehicle module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Plan next level decomposition to explicitly address teleoperation and safety contributions to cycle time.
Strengthen interface definitions to mitigate diffusion risks.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, explicit inclusion of teleoperation and safety DP elements is needed to fully capture cycle time influences and reduce diffusion risks.

revise.why: To incorporate teleoperation and safety aspects explicitly in the design parameters for comprehensive cycle time optimization.

assumptions: Vehicle mechanical and control improvements are primary drivers for cycle time reduction.
Teleoperation and safety modules will cooperate via defined interfaces without excessive delays.
open_risks: Cross-module timing coordination may introduce unforeseen delays.
Safety interventions could conflict with cycle time targets.
evidence_needed: Performance data on vehicle cycle phases under integrated teleoperation and safety conditions.
